description_long: This object consists of a square, white stone planter with a slightly beveled edge, filled with green moss or grass-like ground cover. Rising from the center are several tall, slender bamboo-like stalks, each a pale green with darker green leaves that are long, narrow, and pointed, some with a slight yellowish edge. Among the stalks, a single rounded stone covered in moss is visible, adding a naturalistic touch to the arrangement. The leaves are clustered toward the upper portions of the stalks, creating a dense, vertical foliage effect. The planter itself appears solid and heavy, with a smooth, hard surface and a subtle marbled texture. This arrangement is typical of decorative urban planters found in parks, plazas, or along sidewalks.
description: A white stone planter holds tall bamboo-like stalks with dark green, lanceolate leaves and a mossy stone, creating a lush, vertical floral display.
description_view_0: Front view shows the square white planter, bamboo-like stalks, dense dark green leaves, and a mossy stone clearly visible in the center.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the vertical alignment of the stalks and the layered leaves, with the mossy stone partially obscured behind the foliage.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the planter and the stalks, with leaves overlapping and the stone less visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the mossy stone more prominently among the stalks, with leaves extending outward.
attribute_petal_arrangement: No distinct petals are visible; the arrangement consists of elongated leaves in a whorled and overlapping pattern around the bamboo-like stalks.
